title: Dense Solo Australian Lemongrass Tagine
description: A satisfying, self-contained solo dinner that showcases the unique flavors of lemongrass and the comfort of a traditional tagine.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the bottom of a traditional Australian tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for a further 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cumin powder and paprika powder and stir for 1 minute to release their flavors.
Add the lamb shoulder cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the chopped lemongrass, chicken broth, honey, and lemon juice to the tagine. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover the tagine and cook over low heat for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt to taste, then garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
Serve the tagine hot over rice or with crusty Australian bread.
Optional: Serve with a side of steamed Australian vegetables, such as broccoli or carrots.
To store leftovers, let the tagine cool, then refrigerate or freeze for up to 3 days.
To reheat, gently warm the tagine over low heat, adding a little water if the sauce has thickened too much.
